one_time_description: Initial settlement payment received subsequent to year-end related to Federal Court approved drinking water advisory class action settlement (December 22, 2021)
economic_notes: First Nation located in James Bay region of Ontario. Operates under co-management arrangement per ISC directives. COVID-19 impacted operations including periodic closures, changes in program delivery, delays in infrastructure/capital projects, and increased pandemic-specific funding. Committed to $2,061,464 contract for construction of sewage pumping station. Holds one unit in Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership (OFNLP) receiving 1.7% of provincial gross gaming revenues for community development, health, education, cultural and economic development.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to omission of budget comparison information required by Canadian public sector accounting standards (same basis as prior year qualification). First Nation was operating under ISC co-management arrangement. Financial statements signed January 22, 2024 for fiscal year ended March 31, 2022 — significant delay in filing, also noted as non-compliant with First Nations Financial Transparency Act. Drinking water settlement of $500,000 received subsequent to year-end is a notable contingent/one-time item. OFNLP treated as government business enterprise (equity method, nominal value not recorded). Restricted cash includes CMHC social housing reserves ($268,212) and Ottawa Trust Fund ($6,170). Social housing replacement reserve shortfall of $92,360. Capital lease obligations of $60,977 for equipment. Segmented disclosure shows OFNLP as separate segment with $(283,125) deficit after $991,012 transfer out. ISC funding of $11,170,105 per segmented disclosure used for isc_funding; total_government_transfers estimated including ISC, FNHIB, other provincial/federal, CMHC, NAN, Mushkegowuk, excluding OSR items (rental, gasoline sales, other revenue, gain/loss on disposal).
